notes: THE_BAR — Graustark regulars. The surviving D'Ambrosio son posts. Sammy posts (rare now; his Sundays are at the cemetery). Hector when he has news. Sprint 2 fills it out to 6 threads.

threads.subject: Friday menu · the andouille is on

op.body:
Friday menu is up at the storefront. Hector's andouille with the dirty rice that Sammy talked him out of for two years and finally talked him into. Two seatings: 6:00 and 8:30. Mom's coming for the 6. The dish is on.

— T.

replies.body: I'll be the 8:30. Save me booth four.
I'll have Faith II bring the bones home for Tuesday's broth.

threads.subject: anyone heard from <handle> in the last two years?

op.body:
Sammy asked me Friday if I'd heard from <handle> lately. I haven't. Has anyone here? Last I knew he was up the river somewhere.

— T.

replies.body: He's still there. He runs a small board now. The dial is 5550107 if anyone wants to reach out. Read carefully.

threads.subject: Sammy at the cemetery · the third Sunday

op.body:
Quiet day. The boys came to the storefront late. Sammy went straight to the cemetery from the bus. He sat with Pop for two hours. Mom came at five with the flowers from the back garden.

I'm closing the kitchen tomorrow for inventory. Hector's good with that. He's got his own list.

— T.

Hector's boy got into LSU. Acceptance came yesterday. Hector made the dirty rice for the staff and didn't say anything for an hour. The boy starts in late August.

If you want to congratulate him, do it at the storefront, not in the kitchen. He won't take it in the kitchen.

— T.

replies.body: I'll come for dinner Friday. The 8:30. I'll bring the bourbon.
I have a chair in the shop I built for the kid in '88 when he was four. He didn't take it then. He'll take it now.

threads.subject: the boys finished the back fence

op.body:
Sammy and the boys finished the back fence Saturday. Cedar pickets, half-rail caps, the way Pop did it on the kitchen lot in '78. Sammy did the gate himself. He hung it level. Mom stood at the back step and watched and didn't say anything.

Hector's making the gumbo Friday. — T.

threads.subject: a kid named Mark · asking about Sammy

op.body:
A kid named Mark came by Friday and ordered the catfish and ate all of it and tipped Faith II five on a nine-dollar plate. He's been around the lumber yard a week now. He's asked JF twice if he can meet Sammy. He's asked me once. He didn't ask Mom.

If anyone knows where this kid is from, the storefront wants to know before I make a call on the meeting. — T.

replies.body: He's been polite. He doesn't carry a notebook. He listens. He hasn't asked any question I'd flag. He could still be wrong.
